reasoning:
The bare-catch pattern in $$encryptKey (line 3853-3860) and $$decryptKey (line 3862-3869) of dist/index.node.js is real: both wrap their bodies in catch { throw new Error('Error during encryption/decryption') } with no bound variable, discarding internal exception detail. However, the Bleichenbacher/padding-oracle theory in the finding is invalidated by the actual padding implementation.

The PKCS#1 v1.5 encUnPad (lines 3031-3064) is fully constant-time: all padding byte checks accumulate into a single 'bad' flag with no early returns, and there is exactly one 'if (bad) return null' exit at the end. The OAEP encUnPad (lines 2917-2950) is likewise constant-time, using constantTimeEqual for the lHash check and branchless loops throughout. The Engine.decrypt method (lines 3389-3409) further accumulates the null return from encUnPad into a 'bad' flag before throwing 'Decryption failed', so all padding-failure paths converge to a single throw before reaching the catch block in $$decryptKey.

The catch block therefore swallows three distinct error messages ('Incorrect data or key', 'Engine: RSA primitive returned oversize integer', 'Decryption failed') into 'Error during decryption', which is an information-loss issue but introduces no new timing channel. The padding validation is constant-time before any exception is raised.

The library explicitly acknowledges the residual binary valid/invalid oracle in PKCS#1 v1.5 (comment at line 3025-3029: 'Full Bleichenbacher mitigation... would require session-key plumbing and an API change... This closes only the internal differential timing oracle; the valid/invalid binary oracle inherent to PKCS#1 v1.5 remains — use OAEP for untrusted ciphertexts.'). The default encryption scheme is OAEP (line 3267: DEFAULT_ENCRYPTION_SCHEME = 'pkcs1_oaep'), which is not vulnerable to this class of attack.

Additionally, the finding references a non-existent file path (dist/src/index.d.ts); the actual files are dist/index.node.js and dist/index.node.d.ts.

The error-swallowing is a code-quality defect that hinders debugging, but it does not create a new padding oracle or timing channel beyond what the library already documents and mitigates.
